summary:
The superficial temporal artery is most frequently associated with palpability near the anterior border of the masseter, despite lying primarily along the lateral aspects of the head. Clinically, it can be felt extending forward beyond the zygomatic arch and may appear contiguous with the masseter’s anterior margin during physical examination.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to two-sentence requirement focusing only on direct clinical relevance.)
